textByHashes.d58c28fd9023003014ff0f31c3d2b35d67975cad: I'm sorry, but I'm currently doing some research. Could you talk to me later, please?
textByHashes.a1451d23a4b1571e75856a9ebd197c6c2bad2a7e: Good day, madam. Are you interested in the history of this area?
textByHashes.39c1f4a8d223e3a9799d13ff008da17b640407d3: Good day, sir. Are you interested in the history of this area?
textByHashes.7f3306b92486a1ff08eaa53654e5a56e588efd06: I am a librarian from the great library of Menaphos. I left to travel the desert and learn more of its history.
textByHashes.bfc09607c27a998dc3ffbb530a347907b6b22118: Enakhra? I have read only a little about her. She was one of the Mahjarrat who arrived in Gielinor many thousands of years ago.
textByHashes.3649ee5a9ed1fcba4c0e930f65494e5954637e31: She supported Zamorak when he tried to gain power, and she apparently built a great temple in his honour somewhere in the desert.
textByHashes.713609c3efbd32c82d97676a20f182224cbb9d1d: However, it is said that when she showed it to him, he was not impressed, and told her that he would rather have a weapon that could slay gods.
textByHashes.89e3940b9c53ed3c53db8fdf88053caf4e4cf9bd: That is the last anyone knows of what happened to her, and it took place several thousand years ago.
textByHashes.294888651bbbc1112bcc984a9d5ce4072a3d3c71: An interesting choice of topic. Akthanakos is a Mahjarrat who was apparently worshipped as a god for a while in these parts. That was a long time ago, of course.
textByHashes.f891f6e52e0f3f5a398a096b0a0e578191fbd47e: Unlike many of his kin, he was against Zamorak's rise to power. Perhaps that's why he ended up out here. Zamorak has little sway in the desert.
textByHashes.c48b6ef320a82d6478ffaae5c4092b12c81dd7fe: Whatever his reasons, he clearly took a liking to living here. He took on the head of a camel and even made an amulet that would allow him to understand their kind.
textByHashes.006f5e8fcd77398c34fd8101845ca3762299eb5b: Using it, he even trained some of the native camels to fight for him. Their name, 'Ugthanki', is derived from his.
textByHashes.8250fb99a0c925df1ce6dc02408021df2e2200b6: Since he disappeared, however, it is said that the Ugthanki became more violent and started attacking travellers.
textByHashes.dbdcb9c26cfc8fdbe67a581d599871c0cd0b40b2: It's good to meet someone so eager to learn.
textByHashes.7af95a1eddd47384a3dd65bc6d389d521d986afd: Now, what would you like to hear about?
textByHashes.4acd3e4bae2174ee907b0e76ded9c7efe4c72876: I have read only a little about her. She was one of the Mahjarrat who arrived in Gielinor many thousands of years ago.
textByHashes.4aec61fa207e66208809565e676174d8db7b2272: She supported Zamorak when he tried to gain power, but her attempts to gain his favour failed, and she retreated to a temple she had built.
textByHashes.713eb15246014451f173e87e80e5a6fc69a034a1: Like Enakhra, his rival, Akthanakos came to Gielinor with the other Mahjarrat. He took on the aspect of one of the local gods, and is usually drawn with a camel's head.
textByHashes.59b8a38d84636dbea5c355e4c0eff89e85fce4ae: He was against Zamorak's rise to power, but the last anyone knows of him was that he and Enakhra were thinking of forgetting their rivalry.
textByHashes.80eb7b0489de78c58c7e68ced6b1a0e286bd6307: Well, my sources say that Enakhra tried to gain favour with Zamorak by building him a temple somewhere in the desert.
textByHashes.159ac15160dea2a3d279bc379f3657304c57f877: It is said that when she showed it to him, he was not impressed, and told her that he would rather have a weapon that could slay gods than a temple.
textByHashes.410fac551ec5efa899eb2a0faf8f0a37ddd63e4e: After that, it seems that Enakhra retreated to that temple. If she hasn't died, she's been there ever since.
textByHashes.9f6247a7c5da6fa3e6bc61e986cb04cf478a35d7: I'm afraid I've only heard rumours of it, but here's what I know.
textByHashes.40d8a4b4c5b1e2adf4a16173ea30bdfa73373977: When Akthanakos came to Gielinor and took on the aspect of a man with a camel's head, he made an amulet that would allow him to understand camels.
textByHashes.46adf70c664fa8306566873636a799ec043ca5dc: Because those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.
textByHashes.8554ba957b1c7d59ddf4c4c62b1f09794583c5d8: Knowing something about history can prevent you from making mistakes that people have made before, you see.
textByHashes.17b2ca9c5cd3320e7e21ae067c2211925d1508bc: Unfortunately, the plague in Sophanem prevents me from returning to the library.
